patient_profile:
This 28-year-old software engineer has been feeling overwhelmed with work demands, juggling a tight project deadline and a recent change in management style that's making them question their job security. They've also been struggling to find time for self-care, sacrificing sleep to meet the demands of their role. On top of this, they've experienced some relationship stress with their partner, who's been away on business trips, leaving them feeling lonely and disconnected.

Despite these challenges, they're trying to maintain a sense of normalcy by continuing to prioritize hobbies like hiking and cooking, but even those activities have become more stressful lately as they worry about having enough time for themselves. Overall, they feel like they're just trying to keep their head above water most days.

conversation.content: Hi there, I'm glad you're here to talk about what's been going on lately. Can you tell me a little bit about yourself and why you decided to reach out for some support?
Yeah...I guess I just feel like I've got my head above water most days, but it's definitely an effort. Work has been crazy busy lately and I'm trying to juggle a lot of responsibilities.
That sounds challenging. Can you tell me more about what's been going on at work that's got you feeling so overwhelmed?

Well, my manager changed their approach recently and it's thrown everything off kilter. I'm used to having a lot of autonomy, but now it feels like there are a million rules and regulations to follow.
I see. It sounds like that's causing you some stress. Have you been able to find any time for yourself outside of work?

Honestly, no...not really. I've been sacrificing sleep to get everything done on time and it feels like my partner is always away on business trips so even our usual relaxing time together has become stressful.
I'm sorry to hear that. It sounds like you're feeling a bit disconnected from your partner and also struggling with sleep. How are you taking care of yourself when you do have some downtime?
Well, I used to love hiking on the weekends, but lately it's been more about getting out there quickly so I can get back home and try to catch up on work.
I understand. It sounds like even your hobbies are starting to feel like a chore. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or energy levels?
Actually, yeah...I've been eating more than usual because I'm just too exhausted to cook most nights. And some days it feels like I need an IV of coffee just to get going.
That sounds really tough. It's great that you're acknowledging these changes in your appetite and energy levels. How about your mood? Have you noticed any shifts?
Honestly, some days I just feel like crying...or maybe it's more like feeling numb. Like, nothing seems to be going right and I just want someone to talk to.

I'm here for you and listening. It sounds like you're feeling a bit disconnected from your emotions too. Can you tell me about your relationships with others? Are there any changes or concerns you have?
Yeah...my partner's been away so much that I feel like we're just going through the motions when they are home. It's hard to connect and it feels like we're drifting apart.
That sounds really tough for you. It's understandable that you'd be feeling lonely and disconnected from your partner. How do you think this is affecting your overall well-being?
Honestly, I feel like I'm just trying to survive some days. Like, my body feels like it's constantly running on fumes and my mind is always racing.

I hear you. It sounds like you're feeling really drained and overwhelmed. Let's take a step back and look at what we've talked about so far. Can you identify any patterns or themes that stand out to you?
